translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Paul, an apostle (not from men, nor through man, but through Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead),

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and all the brothers  who are with me, to the assemblies of Galatia:

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Grace to you and peace from God the Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us out of this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I marvel that you are so quickly deserting him who called you in the grace of Christ to a different “good news”;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and there isn’t another “good news.” Only there are some who trouble you, and want to pervert the Good News of Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But even though we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you any “good news” other than that which we preached to you, let him be cursed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As we have said before, so I now say again: if any man preaches to you any “good news” other than that which you received, let him be cursed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For am I now seeking the favor of men, or of God? Or am I striving to please men? For if I were still pleasing men, I wouldn’t be a servant of Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I make known to you, brothers, concerning the Good News which was preached by me, that it is not according to man.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For neither did I receive it from man, nor was I taught it, but it came to me through revelation of Jesus Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you have heard of my way of living in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the assembly of God, and ravaged it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I advanced in the Jews’ religion beyond many of my own age among my countrymen, being more exceedingly zealous for the traditions of my fathers.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when it was the good pleasure of God, who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me through his grace,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the Gentiles, I didn’t immediately confer with flesh and blood,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me, but I went away into Arabia. Then I returned to Damascus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Peter, and stayed with him fifteen days.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But of the other apostles I saw no one, except James, the Lord’s brother.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now about the things which I write to you, behold,  before God, I’m not lying.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then I came to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I was still unknown by face to the assemblies of Judea which were in Christ,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but they only heard: “He who once persecuted us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: And they glorified God in me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Then after a period of fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus also with me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I went up by revelation, and I laid before them the Good News which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately before those who were respected, for fear that I might be running, or had run, in vain.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But not even Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This was because of the false brothers secretly brought in, who stole in to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: to whom we gave no place in the way of subjection, not for an hour, that the truth of the Good News might continue with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But from those who were reputed to be important (whatever they were, it makes no difference to me; God doesn’t show partiality to man) — they, I say, who were respected imparted nothing to me,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but to the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the Good News for the uncircumcision, even as Peter with the Good News for the circumcision

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: (for he who appointed Peter to the apostleship of the circumcision appointed me also to the Gentiles);

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: and when they perceived the grace that was given to me, James and Cephas and John, they who were reputed to be pillars, gave to me and Barnabas the right hand of fellowship, that we should go to the Gentiles, and they to the circumcision.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They only asked us to remember the poor — which very thing I was also zealous to do.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when Peter came to Antioch, I resisted him to his face, because he stood condemned.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For before some people came from James, he ate with the Gentiles. But when they came, he drew back and separated himself, fearing those who were of the circumcision.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: And the rest of the Jews joined him in his hypocrisy; so that even Barnabas was carried away with their hypocrisy.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when I saw that they didn’t walk uprightly according to the truth of the Good News, I said to Peter before them all, “If you, being a Jew, live as the Gentiles do, and not as the Jews do, why do you compel the Gentiles to live as the Jews do?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: “We, being Jews by nature, and not Gentile sinners,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: yet knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ, even we believed in Christ Jesus, that we might be justified by faith in Christ, and not by the works of the law, because no flesh will be justified by the works of the law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if, while we sought to be justified in Christ, we ourselves also were found sinners, is Christ a servant of sin? Certainly not!

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For if I build up again those things which I destroyed, I prove myself a law-breaker.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For I, through the law, died to the law, that I might live to God.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have been crucified with Christ, and it is no longer I that live, but Christ lives in me. That life which I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself up for me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I don’t reject the grace of God. For if righteousness is through the law, then Christ died for nothing!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Foolish Galatians, who has bewitched you not to obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was openly portrayed among you as crucified?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I just want to learn this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Are you so foolish? Having begun in the Spirit, are you now completed in the flesh?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Did you suffer so many things in vain, if it is indeed in vain?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: He therefore who supplies the Spirit to you, and does miracles among you, does he do it by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Even as Abraham “believed God, and it was counted to him for righteousness.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Know therefore that those who are of faith, the same are children of Abraham.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The Scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, preached the Good News beforehand to Abraham, saying, “In you all the nations will be blessed.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So then, those who are of faith are blessed with the faithful Abraham.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For as many as are of the works of the law are under a curse. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who doesn’t continue in all things that are written in the book of the law, to do them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now that no man is justified by the law before God is evident, for, “The righteous will live by faith.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The law is not of faith, but, “The man who does them will live by them.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law, having become a curse for us. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree,”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Christ Jesus; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Brothers, speaking of human terms, though it is only a man’s covenant, yet when it has been confirmed, no one makes it void, or adds to it.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his offspring.  He doesn’t say, “To descendants ”, as of many, but as of one, “To your offspring ”,  which is Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now I say this. A covenant confirmed beforehand by God in Christ, the law, which came four hundred thirty years after, does not annul, so as to make the promise of no effect.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For if the inheritance is of the law, it is no more of promise; but God has granted it to Abraham by promise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What then is the law? It was added because of transgressions, until the offspring  should come to whom the promise has been made. It was ordained through angels by the hand of a mediator.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now a mediator is not between one, but God is one.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Is the law then against the promises of God? Certainly not! For if there had been a law given which could make alive, most certainly righteousness would have been of the law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the Scriptures imprisoned all things under sin, that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But before faith came, we were kept in custody under the law, confined for the faith which should afterwards be revealed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So that the law has become our tutor to bring us to Christ, that we might be justified by faith.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a tutor.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you are all children of God, through faith in Christ Jesus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free man, there is neither male nor female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If you are Christ’s, then you are Abraham’s offspring  and heirs according to promise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I say that so long as the heir is a child, he is no different from a bondservant, though he is lord of all;

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but is under guardians and stewards until the day appointed by the father.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So we also, when we were children, were held in bondage under the elemental principles of the world.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But when the fullness of the time came, God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: that he might redeem those who were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: And because you are children, God sent out the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, “Abba,  Father!”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So you are no longer a bondservant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However at that time, not knowing God, you were in bondage to those who by nature are not gods.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, why do you turn back again to the weak and miserable elemental principles, to which you desire to be in bondage all over again?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You observe days, months, seasons, and years.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I am afraid for you, that I might have wasted my labor for you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I beg you, brothers, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You did me no wrong,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but you know that because of weakness of the flesh I preached the Good News to you the first time.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: That which was a temptation to you in my flesh, you didn’t despise nor reject; but you received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: What was the blessing you enjoyed? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have plucked out your eyes and given them to me.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: They zealously seek you in no good way. No, they desire to alienate you, that you may seek them.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But it is always good to be zealous in a good cause, and not only when I am present with you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: My little children, of whom I am again in travail until Christ is formed in you —

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: but I could wish to be present with you now, and to change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Tell me, you that desire to be under the law, don’t you listen to the law?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the servant, and one by the free woman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However, the son by the servant was born according to the flesh, but the son by the free woman was born through promise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: These things contain an allegory, for these are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children to bondage, which is Hagar.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and answers to the Jerusalem that exists now, for she is in bondage with her children.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the Jerusalem that is above is free, which is the mother of us all.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For it is written, “Rejoice, you barren who don’t bear. Break out and shout, you that don’t travail. For more are the children of the desolate than of her who has a husband.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now we, brothers, as Isaac was, are children of promise.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But as then, he who was born according to the flesh persecuted him who was born according to the Spirit, so also it is now.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: However what does the Scripture say? “Throw out the servant and her son, for the son of the servant will not inherit with the son of the free woman.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So then, brothers, we are not children of a servant, but of the free woman.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Stand firm therefore in the liberty by which Christ has made us free, and don’t be entangled again with a yoke of bondage.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Behold, I, Paul, tell you that if you receive circumcision, Christ will profit you nothing.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Yes, I testify again to every man who receives circumcision, that he is a debtor to do the whole law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You are alienated from Christ, you who desire to be justified by the law. You have fallen away from grace.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For we, through the Spirit, by faith wait for the hope of righteousness.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision amounts to anything, nor uncircumcision, but faith working through love.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: You were running well! Who interfered with you that you should not obey the truth?

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: This persuasion is not from him who calls you.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: A little yeast grows through the whole lump.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I have confidence toward you in the Lord that you will think no other way. But he who troubles you will bear his judgment, whoever he is.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I, brothers, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still persecuted? Then the stumbling block of the cross has been removed.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: I wish that those who disturb you would cut themselves off.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For you, brothers, were called for freedom. Only don’t use your freedom for gain to the flesh, but through love be servants to one another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the whole law is fulfilled in one word, in this: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if you bite and devour one another, be careful that you don’t consume one another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you won’t fulfill the lust of the flesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For the flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; and these are contrary to one another, that you may not do the things that you desire.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Now the deeds of the flesh are obvious, which are: adultery, sexual immorality, uncleanness, lustfulness,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: idolatry, sorcery, hatred, strife, jealousies, outbursts of anger, rivalries, divisions, heresies,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: envy, murders, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these; of which I forewarn you, even as I also forewarned you, that those who practice such things will not inherit God’s Kingdom.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith,

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: gentleness, and self-control. Against such things there is no law.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh with its passions and lusts.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: If we live by the Spirit, let’s also walk by the Spirit.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let’s not become conceited, provoking one another, and envying one another.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Brothers, even if a man is caught in some fault, you who are spiritual must restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness; looking to yourself so that you also aren’t tempted.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Bear one another’s burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For if a man thinks himself to be something when he is nothing, he deceives himself.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But let each man examine his own work, and then he will have reason to boast in himself, and not in someone else.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For each man will bear his own burden.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But let him who is taught in the word share all good things with him who teaches.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Don’t be deceived. God is not mocked, for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For he who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption. But he who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: Let us not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season, if we don’t give up.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: So then, as we have opportunity, let’s do what is good toward all men, and especially toward those who are of the household of the faith.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: See with what large letters I write to you with my own hand.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As many as desire to look good in the flesh, they compel you to be circumcised; only that they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For even they who receive circumcision don’t keep the law themselves, but they desire to have you circumcised, that they may boast in your flesh.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: But far be it from me to boast, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: For in Christ Jesus neither is circumcision anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: As many as walk by this rule, peace and mercy be on them, and on God’s Israel.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: From now on, let no one cause me any trouble, for I bear the marks of the Lord Jesus branded on my body.

translations.name: World English Bible
translations.text: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.
